calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-24-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Set For Trial On Apr-20-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Clark, Esq. of McNamara Ney Beatty LLP, Tel. 925.939.5330, for defendant and moving party;
Peter M. Wucetich, Esq. of the Law Office of Peter M. Wucetich, Tel. 626.432.5420, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: A R G U E D. After argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LINDA CHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted in part and denied in part. Motion denied as to striking Los Angeles County as the proper venue. Defendant fails to present and the Court fails to identify any reason to grant, and finds the request entirely unnecessary. Motion denied as to the prayer for injunctive relief. Defamation is not constitutionally protected speech. Motion is granted as to Plaintiff's requests for attorneys' fees with fifteen days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to state the contractual or statutory basis for the request. Motion denied as to striking punitive damages as the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts amounting to malice and an intent to injure Plaintiff. Motion denied as to striking the defamation cause of action as duplicative of defamation per se. Plaintiff is permitted to assert alternative causes of action. Motion is granted without leave to amend as to the allegations of defamatory statements made prior to March 13, 2012 as barred by the statute of limitations, which is not a curable defect. Motion is denied as to striking the unfair business practices cause of action because it is not merely duplicative of the defamation claims. Motion denied as to striking the cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Defendant sets forth grounds for demurrer to a cause of action rather than grounds to strike. Finally, the Motion is granted as to striking the preliminary injunction cause of action with leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-25-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Clark, Esq. of McNamara Ney Beatty LLP, Tel. 925.939.5330, for defendant and moving party;
Peter M. Wucetich, Esq. of the Law Office of Peter M. Wucetich, Tel. 626.432.5420, for plaintiff and opposing party, by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. After arguments, the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LINDA CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to breach of contract. Plaintiff apparently intended but failed to include a copy of a written agreement as an exhibit to the Complaint. Leave to amend in order to attach the agreement is granted. Demurrer to this cause of action on all other grounds is overruled as Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged each element of a breach of contract claim. Demurrer is overruled as to the defamation and defamation per se causes of action. The Complaint quotes no less than seven allegedly false statements, (Compl. 25-27, 29, 30, 31, 33) and sufficiently alleges each required element. Moreover, the Complaint contains allegations of statements made after March 13, 2012 and therefore the demurrer is overruled as the later statements support the cause of action. Demurrer is sustained as to the unfair business practices cause of action with fifteen days leave to amend to permit Plaintiff to allege an injury in fact and a causal connection between Defendant's UCL violation and Plaintiff's injury. (See Bus. & Prof. Code 17204 as amended by Prop 64.) Demurrer to this cause of action on all other grounds is overruled. Demurrer is also overruled as to the intentional infliction of emotion distress cause of action as the Complaint sufficiently alleges each element. Finally, the Demurrer is sustained as to the prohibitory injunction claim with 15 days to amend the complaint. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
